revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:目黒護國神社 up to rev 102219533
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 目黒護國神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[目黒区]][[五本木]]二丁目20番17号 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 38 | 緯度秒 = 4.1 |経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 21 |地図 = |祭神 = [[日清戦争]]以降の戦没者約2300柱 |神体 = |社格 = 指定外護国神社 |創建 = [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''目黒護國神社'''（めぐろごこくじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[目黒区]]にあった[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。
== 歴史 == === 創建 === [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）3月に創建された。[[台湾日日新報]]社長だった守屋善兵衛（[[1930年]]没）の[[遺族]]より、目黒区に対して[[宅地]]と[[住宅|居宅]]の一部を[[寄付|寄贈]]する申し出があり、その際に「[[英霊|忠霊]]を祀る神社を建設すること等」という条件があったので、当時の目黒区は[[公金|公費]]で神社を創建した<ref name="目黒区">{{Cite book |和書 |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17年度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=目黒区 |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}</ref><ref name="東京新聞">{{Cite news |和書 |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=[[東京新聞]] |date=2008-07-08 |edition=夕刊}}</ref>。
[[第二次世界大戦]]後、[[日本国憲法]]の[[施行]]により、目黒区が神社を運営することができなくなったため（[[政教分離原則]]）、戦没者遺族が管理することになった。[[1958年]]（昭和33年）7月、[[目黒区立図書館|目黒区立守屋図書館]]の改築にあたり神社を敷地内（五本木[[庚申塔]]<ref>[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/shougaigakushuu/bunkasports/rekishibunkazai/gohongi.html 五本木庚申塔]、目黒区</ref>の隣接地）に移転した<ref name="目黒区"/>。[[1959年]]（昭和34年）10月に「目黒護國神社崇敬会」が設立され、目黒区と[[賃貸借|土地賃貸借契約]]（敷地面積198.50[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）を結ぶことになった<ref name="目黒区"/>。
=== 廃社 === ところが、[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）以降、管理者をしていた崇敬会の役員が死去したことにより、崇敬会の活動が休止した。目黒区に払う[[地代]]も滞納するようになり、[[神体|御魂]]であった戦没者名簿も行方不明となった<ref name="東京新聞"/>。
[[2005年]]度（平成17年度）の目黒区の[[外部監査制度|包括外部監査]]で、単に政教分離上の問題だけでなく、建物が老朽化しており安全面でも問題があるとの指摘を受けた<ref name="目黒区"/>。
[[2008年]]（平成20年）7月、隣接する旧目黒区守屋教育会館の解体に伴い撤去された。他神社への合祀や他団体の管理も模索されたというが、折り合いがつかず結局断念することになった<ref name="東京新聞"/>。
なお、目黒護國神社の跡地には[[防災倉庫|防災備蓄倉庫]]が、旧目黒区守屋教育会館の跡地には[[保育所|私立保育園]]（旧目黒区立上目黒保育園の移転民営化<ref>[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画（令和3年度～11年度）]、目黒区</ref>）が建てられた（目黒区立守屋図書館が南側にある）。
== 所在地 == * [[東京都]][[目黒区]][[五本木]]二丁目1186番3（[[地番]]） * 東京都目黒区五本木二丁目20番17号（[[住居表示]]）
== 沿革 == * [[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年） ** 7月 - 守屋善兵衛の[[遺族]]から旧邸宅の寄付申し込み（条件：忠霊を祀る神社を建設すること等） ** 9月 - 寄付受領議決 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年） ** 3月 - 目黒護國神社建立（区費負担） ** 4月 - [[所有権|土地所有権]]移転登記 ** 12月 - 守屋記念館（後の[[目黒区立図書館|目黒区立守屋図書館]]）開館 * [[1947年]]（昭和22年）5月 - [[日本国憲法]][[施行]]により神社は遺族が管理 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）7月 - 守屋図書館改築にあたり神社を敷地内で移転 * [[1959年]]（昭和34年）10月 - 目黒護国神社崇敬会設立 * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）4月 - 土地賃貸借契約を崇敬会と締結 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）9月 - 賃貸料改定契約 * [[1976年]]（昭和51年）4月 - 土地賃貸借契約の更新 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）4月 - 土地賃貸借契約の更新 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）3月 - 土地賃貸借契約の期間満了 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）4月 - 地代の授受を継続 * [[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）2月 - 関係者（遺族会）と話し合い * [[1999年]]（平成11年）1月 - 関係者（崇敬会、遺族会）と話し合い * [[2000年]]（平成13年）3月 - 地代未納につき土地賃貸借契約解除手続き * [[2008年]]（平成20年）7月 - 隣接する旧目黒区立守屋教育会館の解体に伴い撤去
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[目黒区立図書館|目黒区立守屋図書館]] - [[台湾日日新報]]社長・守屋善兵衛の旧邸宅跡（敷地計約3300m<sup>2</sup>）に建てられた[[図書館]]。目黒護國神社も当敷地内に存在した。
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めくろここくしんしや}} [[Category:目黒区の神社|廃めくろここくしんしや]] [[Category:目黒区の歴史]] [[Category:目黒区の建築物|廃めくろここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|廃めくろここくしんしや]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[目黒護國神社]] to [[Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 目黒護國神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[目黒区]][[五本木]]二丁目20番17号 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 38 | 緯度秒 = 4.1 |経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 21 |地図 = |祭神 = [[日清戦争]]以降の戦没者約2300柱 |神体 = |社格 = 指定外護国神社 |創建 = [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''目黒護國神社'''（めぐろごこくじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[目黒区]]にあった[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。
== 歴史 == === 創建 === [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）3月に創建された。[[台湾日日新報]]社長だった守屋善兵衛（[[1930年]]没）の[[遺族]]より、目黒区に対して[[宅地]]と[[住宅|居宅]]の一部を[[寄付|寄贈]]する申し出があり、その際に「[[英霊|忠霊]]を祀る神社を建設すること等」という条件があったので、当時の目黒区は[[公金|公費]]で神社を創建した<ref name="目黒区">{{Cite book |和書 |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17年度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=目黒区 |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}</ref><ref name="東京新聞">{{Cite news |和書 |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=[[東京新聞]] |date=2008-07-08 |edition=夕刊}}</ref>。
[[第二次世界大戦]]後、[[日本国憲法]]の[[施行]]により、目黒区が神社を運営することができなくなったため（[[政教分離原則]]）、戦没者遺族が管理することになった。[[1958年]]（昭和33年）7月、[[目黒区立図書館|目黒区立守屋図書館]]の改築にあたり神社を敷地内（五本木[[庚申塔]]<ref>[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/shougaigakushuu/bunkasports/rekishibunkazai/gohongi.html 五本木庚申塔]、目黒区</ref>の隣接地）に移転した<ref name="目黒区"/>。[[1959年]]（昭和34年）10月に「目黒護國神社崇敬会」が設立され、目黒区と[[賃貸借|土地賃貸借契約]]（敷地面積198.50[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）を結ぶことになった<ref name="目黒区"/>。
=== 廃社 === ところが、[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）以降、管理者をしていた崇敬会の役員が死去したことにより、崇敬会の活動が休止した。目黒区に払う[[地代]]も滞納するようになり、[[神体|御魂]]であった戦没者名簿も行方不明となった<ref name="東京新聞"/>。
[[2005年]]度（平成17年度）の目黒区の[[外部監査制度|包括外部監査]]で、単に政教分離上の問題だけでなく、建物が老朽化しており安全面でも問題があるとの指摘を受けた<ref name="目黒区"/>。
[[2008年]]（平成20年）7月、隣接する旧目黒区守屋教育会館の解体に伴い撤去された。他神社への合祀や他団体の管理も模索されたというが、折り合いがつかず結局断念することになった<ref name="東京新聞"/>。
なお、目黒護國神社の跡地には[[防災倉庫|防災備蓄倉庫]]が、旧目黒区守屋教育会館の跡地には[[保育所|私立保育園]]（旧目黒区立上目黒保育園の移転民営化<ref>[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画（令和3年度～11年度）]、目黒区</ref>）が建てられた（目黒区立守屋図書館が南側にある）。
== 所在地 == * [[東京都]][[目黒区]][[五本木]]二丁目1186番3（[[地番]]） * 東京都目黒区五本木二丁目20番17号（[[住居表示]]）
== 沿革 == * [[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年） ** 7月 - 守屋善兵衛の[[遺族]]から旧邸宅の寄付申し込み（条件：忠霊を祀る神社を建設すること等） ** 9月 - 寄付受領議決 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年） ** 3月 - 目黒護國神社建立（区費負担） ** 4月 - [[所有権|土地所有権]]移転登記 ** 12月 - 守屋記念館（後の[[目黒区立図書館|目黒区立守屋図書館]]）開館 * [[1947年]]（昭和22年）5月 - [[日本国憲法]][[施行]]により神社は遺族が管理 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）7月 - 守屋図書館改築にあたり神社を敷地内で移転 * [[1959年]]（昭和34年）10月 - 目黒護国神社崇敬会設立 * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）4月 - 土地賃貸借契約を崇敬会と締結 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）9月 - 賃貸料改定契約 * [[1976年]]（昭和51年）4月 - 土地賃貸借契約の更新 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）4月 - 土地賃貸借契約の更新 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）3月 - 土地賃貸借契約の期間満了 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）4月 - 地代の授受を継続 * [[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）2月 - 関係者（遺族会）と話し合い * [[1999年]]（平成11年）1月 - 関係者（崇敬会、遺族会）と話し合い * [[2000年]]（平成13年）3月 - 地代未納につき土地賃貸借契約解除手続き * [[2008年]]（平成20年）7月 - 隣接する旧目黒区立守屋教育会館の解体に伴い撤去
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[目黒区立図書館|目黒区立守屋図書館]] - [[台湾日日新報]]社長・守屋善兵衛の旧邸宅跡（敷地計約3300m<sup>2</sup>）に建てられた[[図書館]]。目黒護國神社も当敷地内に存在した。
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めくろここくしんしや}} [[Category:目黒区の神社|廃めくろここくしんしや]] [[Category:目黒区の歴史]] [[Category:目黒区の建築物|廃めくろここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|廃めくろここくしんしや]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. {{citation needed|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Meguro]] [[Category:Former shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Defunct buildings and structures in Tokyo]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}}

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|12=draft}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. {{citation needed|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Meguro]] [[Category:Former shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Defunct buildings and structures in Tokyo]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]]

{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|12=draft}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. {{citation needed|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]]

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. {{citation needed|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, {{ill|Meguro, Tokyo|ja|目黒区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:目黒区の歴史]]

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:目黒区の歴史]] [[Category:目黒区の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:目黒区の神社]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]
