Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[World War II]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の Image Requests]] [[Category:Shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の Image Requests]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の Image Requests]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[:en:Showa era|Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[:en:Separation of church and state|Separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:現存しない東京都区部の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:目黒区の Image Requests]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852432]] Wikidata name: Meguro gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[:en:Showa era|Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[:en:Separation of church and state|Separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852432]] Wikidata name: Meguro gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|35.634174|N|139.688873|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=|1=Gohongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372479}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine | prefecture= {{ill|Gohongi|qid=Q11372479}}; {{ill|Goppongi 2-chome|qid=Q122687454}}; {{ill|Meguro-ku|qid=Q233903}}; {{ill|Tokyo|qid=Q1490}} }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=|1=Gohongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372479}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[:en:Showa era|Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[:en:Separation of church and state|Separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=|1=Meguro Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683396}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]][[every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=|1=Meguro Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683396}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} [[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[sn:eguro Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q106852432}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852432]] Wikidata name: Meguro gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|35.634174|N|139.688873|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=|1=Gohongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372479}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine | prefecture= {{ill|Gohongi|qid=Q11372479}}; {{ill|Goppongi 2-chome|qid=Q122687454}}; {{ill|Meguro-ku|qid=Q233903}}; {{ill|Tokyo|qid=Q1490}} }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=|1=Gohongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372479}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[:en:Showa era|Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[:en:Separation of church and state|Separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=|1=Meguro Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683396}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q106852432}}

In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=|1=Meguro Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683396}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q106852432}}

[[ja:目黒護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852432]] Wikidata name: Meguro gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Meguro Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|35.634174|N|139.688873|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Meguro Gokoku Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=目黒区}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|35|38|4.04|N|139|41|21.24|E|format=dms}}|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} | location = {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=|1=Gohongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372479}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | latitude = 35.634456 | longitude = 139.689233 | deities = Approximately 2,300 war dead from the [[First Sino-Japanese War]] onward | founded = 1941 (Showa 16) | festival = | structure = | class = Non-designated Gokoku Shrine | prefecture= {{ill|Gohongi|WD=Q11372479}}; {{ill|Goppongi 2-chome|WD=Q122687454}}; {{ill|Meguro-ku|WD=Q233903}}; {{ill|Tokyo|WD=Q1490}} }} '''Meguro Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|目黒護國神社|1=Meguro Gokoku Shrine|Meguro Gokoku Jinja}}) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in {{ill|Gohongi, Meguro|ja|五本木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohongi, Meguro|qq=|1=Gohongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372479}}, [[:en:Meguro|Meguro, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan. It was a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == === Establishment === The shrine was established in March 1941, during the early [[:en:Showa era|Showa era]], following a donation of residential land and part of a house by the family of Zenbei Moriya, the former president of the Taiwan Nichi Nichi Shinpō newspaper, with the condition that a shrine honoring fallen war heroes be built. The construction of the shrine was funded by public expenses.{{Cite book |url=https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/67/h17.pdf |title=平成17th year 度 包括外部監査の結果報告書 |publisher=Meguro City |date=2006-02 |pages=111-112}}{{Cite news |title=戦没者ほこらひっそり幕 目黒護国神社、管理者なく |newspaper=Tokyo Shimbun |date=2008-07-08 |edition=Evening}} After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] and the implementation of the [[:en:Japanese Constitution|Japanese Constitution]], Meguro City could no longer operate the shrine due to the [[:en:Separation of church and state|Separation of church and state]]. Management was transferred to the bereaved families of war victims. In 1958, the shrine was relocated within the grounds of the Meguro Public Library due to renovations. === Abolition === By 2000, the shrine's managing association had become inactive following the deaths of key members. This resulted in unpaid land rent and the disappearance of the war dead registry. In 2005, an external audit cited not only governance concerns but also safety issues due to the aging structure. In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|qq=|1=Meguro Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683396}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q106852432}}

In July 2008, the shrine was dismantled during the demolition of the adjacent Moriya Educational Hall. Attempts to transfer its management or merge it with other shrines were unsuccessful. The former site of the shrine is now occupied by a [[disaster preparedness storage facility]], while the Moriya Educational Hall was replaced by a private nursery school.[https://www.city.meguro.tokyo.jp/documents/623/mineikakeikaku_1.pdf 区立保育園の民営化に関する計画] - Meguro City == location == * Address: 2-20-17 Gohongi, Meguro City, Tokyo, Japan == Timeline == * 1940 (Showa 15) ''' July: Donation of Zenbei Moriya's former residence with conditions to establish the shrine ''' September: Donation approved * 1941 (Showa 16) ''' March: Shrine established using public funds ''' April: Land ownership registered ''' December: Moriya Memorial Hall (later Meguro Public Library) opened * 1958 (Showa 33): Shrine relocated within the Moriya Memorial Hall grounds during library renovations * 1959 (Showa 34): Meguro Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association formed * 2000 (Heisei 12): Shrine management became inactive * 2008 (Heisei 20): Shrine dismantled == Related Articles == * {{ill|Meguro Public Library|ja|目黒区立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meguro Public Library|1=Meguro Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683396}} - Constructed on the site of Zenbei Moriya's former residence, which included the Meguro Gokoku Shrine. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|目黒護國神社|version=102219533|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Meguro Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:目黒護國神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former buildings in Tokyo wards]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Meguro]] [[Category:History of Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Image requests in Meguro Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Meguro, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q106852432}}
